Document:
[color=#ffff00][head=3]Section 6: Roleplay Rules[/head][/color]
[color=#a4885c]6.1[/color] Your character is a separate entity from you, the player. Your character's actions, feelings, and knowledge in-game should be based solely on the character's experiences rather than your own as the player. [color=#f1c232]★★[/color]
[color=#a4885c]6.2[/color] Playing a character accurately should never result in rulebreaks. Do not intentionally design your character in such a way that they break any other rule by existing. [color=#f1c232]★★[/color]
[color=#a4885c]6.3[/color] If you choose a Command, Security, EMS, or faction role, you are required to participate in the round as that role when necessary. ★
- [color=#a4885c]6.3A[/color] Choosing a job means you are actively playing that role during that shift. Do not change roles in quick succession.
- [color=#a4885c]6.3B[/color] Switching your role to a higher position, such as a Command role, is allowed but only with an RP promotion and not for an OOC reason (e.g. needing to transfer a deed by switching from Mercenary to Overseer or DoC).
- [color=#a4885c]6.3C[/color] Once you take a Command role, you must commit to that role while you are in that shift as that character. Do not rejoin on the same character as a non-Command role in the same round you played Command.
[color=#a4885c]6.4[/color] When players are engaging in roleplay such as hostage-taking, robberies, or otherwise, do not transition to mechanical combat while they are still roleplaying or typing. ★
- [color=#a4885c]6.4A[/color] Leeway is given if the scene is near its conclusion, but all players are expected to engage with it in good faith, even if this puts them at a disadvantage.
[color=#a4885c]6.5[/color] Do not directly refer to internet culture or use netspeak while roleplaying, for example "lol" or "skibidi toilet". ★
